prompt_text:
### Demographics: 52-year-old Asian man

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 12 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after ophthalmology referral for suspected retinopathy
Current symptoms: Occasional fatigue and thirst with nocturia; no DKA/HHS signs; no severe hypoglycemia; Despite repeated counseling on insulin need, the patient strongly refuses injections; shared decision was made to intensify oral therapy and reassess within 2-4 weeks.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 2 (60-89)
Heart failure: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III)
MASH
Body mass index category: Obese Class I
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
Major Depressive Disorder
Psychotic Disorder on Atypical Antipsychotics
Appendicitis (status post appendectomy)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 2 (60-89); HF: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III); ASCVD (No); MASLD: MASH; BMI Category: Obese Class I
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (younger sister)
- Social history: Current smoker (20 cigarettes/day for 10 years; ~10 pack-years); Alcohol 3-4 drinks/week (2-3 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Glimepiride 4mg once daily; Olanzapine (10 mg nightly), Sertraline (50 mg daily), Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily), Ciprofloxacin (500 mg twice daily for 7 days)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 85.3 kg; Height 180.4 cm; BMI 26.2 kg/m²; Blood pressure 140/78 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 10.7%; 9 months ago 11.0%; 6 months ago 11.0%; 3 months ago 11.0%; current 10.0%), FPG (12 months ago 234mg/dL; 9 months ago 287mg/dL; 6 months ago 300mg/dL; 3 months ago 320mg/dL; current 300mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 86.2kg; 9 months ago 83.9kg; 6 months ago 83.8kg; 3 months ago 84.8kg; current 85.3kg), Height (12 months ago 180.4cm; 9 months ago 180.4cm; 6 months ago 180.4cm; 3 months ago 180.4cm; current 180.4cm), BMI (12 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 26.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 26.0kg/m²; current 26.2kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 138mmHg; 9 months ago 140mmHg; 6 months ago 137mmHg; 3 months ago 139mmHg; current 140mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 81mmHg; 9 months ago 82mmHg; 6 months ago 80mmHg; 3 months ago 80mmHg; current 78mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 84U/L; 9 months ago 86U/L; 6 months ago 93U/L; 3 months ago 87U/L; current 85U/L), AST (12 months ago 58U/L; 9 months ago 60U/L; 6 months ago 53U/L; 3 months ago 54U/L; current 53U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 1.0mg/dL; 9 months ago 1.0mg/dL; 6 months ago 1.0mg/dL; 3 months ago 1.0mg/dL; current 1.0mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 76mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 80mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 77mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 78mL/min/1.73m²; current 77mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 58mg/g; 9 months ago 40mg/g; 6 months ago 42mg/g; 3 months ago 41mg/g; current 49mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 90mg/dL; 9 months ago 91mg/dL; 6 months ago 91mg/dL; 3 months ago 91mg/dL; current 88mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 53mg/dL; 9 months ago 50mg/dL; 6 months ago 50mg/dL; 3 months ago 54mg/dL; current 52mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 122mg/dL; 9 months ago 123mg/dL; 6 months ago 139mg/dL; 3 months ago 122mg/dL; current 130mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 16.0g/dL; 9 months ago 16.0g/dL; 6 months ago 16.0g/dL; 3 months ago 15.2g/dL; current 15.0g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited. Strong injection refusal is documented; under shared decision-making, the patient accepts oral regimen adjustment with short-interval reassessment.
Financial context: Patient has standard insurance with moderate copays and a monthly medication budget of ≤ KRW 500,000.
